response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished generalist software engineer with strong distributed-systems, low-latency, and craftsmanship credentials, but the role centers on hands-on senior C++/STL development for industrial automation, which is almost entirely absent from their profile. C++ exposure is limited to ~6 months at National Instruments (2014-2015) and a bachelor thesis; the listed skills do not even include C++. There is no evidence of message-broker/MQTT industrial work, IEC 62443/SDL secure engineering, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.). Despite relevant strengths in concurrency and high-throughput systems, the core technical and domain requirements are not met.
strengths: Deep experience with high-throughput, low-latency, multi-threaded/async systems (millions of req/s)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, testing, mentoring/teaching
Broad architecture and full-stack background including event-driven and distributed platforms
concerns: Minimal and dated C++/STL experience; C++ not even listed among core skills
No industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, or relevant protocol (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA) exposure
Recent work is TypeScript/Node/DLang-focused, misaligned with a hands-on senior C++ mandate
